message:
demo_showcase: integrate Animator for sidebar focus transitions

App struct integration:
- Import Animator from crate::data::animation
- Add animator: Animator field to App struct for UI transitions
- Reference bead bd-30md for animation coordination tracking

Initialization:
- Capture config.animations flag before constructing App
- Initialize Animator::new(animations) respecting user's animation
  preferences from configuration

Sidebar focus animations:
- Tab toggle: trigger animate("sidebar_focus", 1.0) when focusing,
  animate("sidebar_focus", 0.0) when unfocusing
- Escape unfocus: animate("sidebar_focus", 0.0) when sidebar loses
  focus via Escape key
- Smooth visual transition between focused/unfocused states

These animations respect the --no-animations flag and REDUCE_MOTION
environment variable, falling back to instant transitions when
animations are disabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-366a","title":"Integration Tests: glamour + lipgloss styling","description":"Integration tests for glamour markdown with lipgloss styles.

## Purpose
Verify glamour correctly uses lipgloss for styled output
and that styles compose without corruption.

## Test Requirements

### Style Application
- [ ] glamour Style maps to lipgloss Styles
- [ ] Custom themes apply correctly
- [ ] Default themes render expected output

### Rendering Integration
- [ ] Markdown elements get correct styles
- [ ] Code blocks get syntax highlighting colors
- [ ] Nested styles (bold in italic) work
- [ ] Links have distinct styling

### Width Handling
- [ ] glamour respects lipgloss width
- [ ] Word wrap accounts for ANSI codes
- [ ] Padding/margin calculations correct

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Very wide content with styles
- [ ] Many nested style changes
- [ ] Styles across line breaks

## Success Criteria
- Styled markdown renders identically to Go charm
- No style leakage between elements
- Width calculations match expected","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:49.408003481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:20.534265045Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:52:20.534133109Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","integration","lipgloss","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-15c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:25.675118001Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:27.128987696Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-366a","depends_on_id":"bd-3nyf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:49.433434947Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- Propagate size to pages/components (viewport/table/list widths/heights).

-{"id":"bd-39hl","title":"Dashboard: uptime stopwatch + incident countdown widget","description":"Exercise bubbles time components in a realistic, high-polish dashboard context.

Why:
- `bubbles::stopwatch` and `bubbles::timer` are core "real app" primitives (uptime, ETAs, countdowns).
- Time widgets also stress the update loop (tick scheduling, formatting, focus/reduce-motion behavior).

Widgets to implement:
- Uptime (stopwatch):
  - displayed as `DD:HH:MM:SS` (or similar) with compact formatting
  - clearly indicates running/paused state (pause if unfocused is optional)
- Incident SLA / next maintenance window (timer countdown):
  - counts down to an upcoming event
  - visual emphasis as it nears zero (color + subtle animation)

Integration:
- Drive updates via bubbletea tick/every (no manual sleeps).
- Respect global toggles:
  - reduce-motion / `--no-animations` (no flashy pulsing)
  - focus/blur (optionally throttle when unfocused)

Acceptance:
- Widgets look polished and update smoothly.
- No excessive CPU usage (tick frequency is reasonable).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:24.377240967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:24.071286192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","stopwatch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:24.396412480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:26.892799063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:28.950716964Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-39my","title":"demo_showcase data: async job runner demo (AsyncCmd)","description":"When the `bubbletea/async` feature is enabled, demonstrate a realistic async workload pipeline using `AsyncCmd` (non-blocking background work that produces messages).

Why:
- Async is a headline capability: it proves TUIs can do real I/O work without blocking input.
- The showcase should prove both *correctness* (no races/panics) and *ergonomics* (clean update logic).

What to demonstrate (in-app):
- I/O-like operations:
  - “fetch metrics”, “deploy service”, “load docs index”, “export logs”, etc.
  - each operation returns a message that updates state + triggers UI feedback
- Concurrency:
  - multiple AsyncCmds can run concurrently (batch)
  - results reconcile safely into the model
- Cancellation semantics (best-effort):
  - bubbletea AsyncCmd itself doesn’t inherently cancel tasks; demonstrate cancellation via:
    - per-job generation IDs / cancellation tokens
    - ignoring late results for canceled jobs
    - UI shows “canceled” and stops progress updates

Implementation notes:
- Feature-gate the async runner with `cfg(feature = "async")` and provide a sync fallback path so the demo still works without async.
- Prefer deterministic delays in test mode (e.g., fixed durations or seeded RNG) so E2E is stable.

Acceptance:
- Async mode feels like a real app doing background work while remaining responsive.
- No input blocking; no terminal corruption; no runaway task spawning.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - async pipeline updates state correctly (use tokio test + deterministic time where feasible)
  - cancellation token/generation logic ignores late results
  - batch/sequence composition behaves as expected
- E2E:
  - at least one scenario runs with async enabled (can be a separate CI job if needed) and asserts job progression + completion.

Notes:
- Keep the API surface small: most of the complexity should live in demo_showcase domain state, not in glue.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyGlen","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:52.895189964Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:59:00.694234598Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:52.911117280Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:53.253293494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39my","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:51.982805847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39te","title":"Unit tests: println/printf gating (only when no-alt-screen)","description":"Add unit tests ensuring the demo_showcase uses bubbletea `println/printf` intentionally and only in the right mode.

Why:
- `println/printf` is a powerful feature but can easily create messy output if used in alt-screen.
- We want a crisp, deterministic policy: only emit unmanaged lines when the user explicitly opts in.

Test coverage:
- When Config indicates alt-screen is enabled (default):
  - events that would normally log/notify do *not* produce `PrintLineMsg` commands.
- When Config indicates `--no-alt-screen`:
  - the same events do produce `bubbletea::println/printf` output (i.e., `PrintLineMsg`).

Implementation hint:
- Keep the policy in a small helper (e.g., `maybe_print_line(config, msg) -> Cmd`) so tests can execute the Cmd and assert on the returned Message type.

Acceptance:
- Tests are deterministic and do not require a real terminal.
- Failures clearly indicate which mode produced unexpected output.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.095304097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:14.125758892Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:01:14.125658425Z","close_reason":"Unit tests for println/printf gating complete. Created tests/println_gating_tests.rs with 9 tests covering deployment event command production, bubbletea runtime gating documentation, command execution, and config alt_screen settings. All tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.120238122Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.110807003Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39te","depends_on_id":"bd-7iul","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:13:29.115944759Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-39zu","title":"demo_showcase: unified theming + styling system (lipgloss)","description":"Make the demo visually cohesive and "turned up to 11" by building a real theming system on top of lipgloss.

Deliverables:
- ThemeContext-driven styles (semantic slots: primary/success/warn/error/muted/border/etc).
- Live theme switching (multiple presets + maybe custom) without UI glitches.
- Shared layout helpers: app chrome, cards, tables, badges, buttons, toasts.

Acceptance:
- Every page uses the same style primitives; switching theme updates the whole app.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.727815830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.746609025Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","lipgloss","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39zu","depends_on_id":"bd-yqh4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:05.746566305Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
